Article Title: 'Political vendetta': Congress vows to block Bill seeking automatic removal of PM, CMs after 30 days in jail
Article Text:
What is the Bill?

The Congress on Sunday said it would ‘strongly oppose’ the proposed Constitution amendment Bill that seeks the automatic removal of the Prime Minister, chief ministers and ministers if they remain in judicial custody for 30 consecutive days in cases involving serious criminal offences.The party also expressed confidence that the BJP-led government would fail to secure the two-thirds majority required to pass the legislation during the Monsoon Session of Parliament beginning July 20. PTI reported that a parliamentary panel examining the Bills is expected to adopt its report on July 17.The proposed Constitution (130th Amendment) Bill provides for the automatic removal of the prime minister, chief ministers and ministers from office if they remain in judicial custody for 30 consecutive days in cases involving offences punishable with imprisonment of more than five years. Introduced by Union Home Minister Amit Shah in August 2025, the Bill was referred to a Joint Parliamentary Committee, which is expected to submit its report during the upcoming Monsoon Session.Congress general secretary Jairam Ramesh alleged that the legislation was designed to facilitate "political harassment" of the Opposition."They will try to bring the 130th Constitutional Amendment Bill, which we are going to oppose.It is a dangerous Bill that was introduced in August of 2025 and was subsequently referred to a Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC), which most opposition parties boycotted," Ramesh told PTI.Explaining the provisions of the Bill, he said, "The Bill states that if a minister is in jail for 30 consecutive days for a criminal offence punishable by more than five years, then on the 31st day, he or she will be dismissed."Calling the proposal unprecedented, Ramesh argued, "This is extraordinary. I mean, court proceedings are still going on. In India, you are presumed innocent until proven guilty. We all know how the probe agencies have been functioning in the (Prime Minister Narendra) Modi-Home Minister Amit Shah regime."Describing the proposal as politically motivated, he said, "This is nothing but political vendetta" and added, "It is to ensure political harassment of your adversaries."The Congress leader also said the party would oppose the legislation if it is brought before Parliament during the Monsoon Session."They may try to reintroduce it in this session, during the Monsoon Session... We are going to oppose it, we are going to oppose the delimitation Bill which came during a Special Session on April 16 and the home minister was humiliated beyond measure on April 17 when he got only 298 MPs for the Constitutional amendment on delimitation, whereas he required 352 votes," he said.Expressing confidence that the government lacked the numbers to amend the Constitution, Ramesh asserted, "I will say with confidence that Mr Amit Shah and Mr Narendra Modi will not get two-thirds majority if they try to push the 130th Constitutional Amendment Bill on the dismissal of ministers, CMs and the delimitation Bill which they lost on April 17."He further alleged that Shah had attempted to engineer splits within Opposition parties."He split the TMC and the Shiv Sena (UBT). Yes, those splits are a challenge for us. It is a setback for the Shiv Sena (UBT), it is a setback for the TMC, and it is a setback for the opposition. But our unity and solidarity continues," Ramesh said.The remarks come days before the Joint Parliamentary Committee is expected to adopt its reports on the Constitution (130th Amendment) Bill, the Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation (Amendment) Bill and the Government of Union Territories (Amendment) Bill. The committee, chaired by BJP MP Aparajita Sarangi, has consulted constitutional experts, retired judges, lawyers, government officials and political leaders as part of its deliberations.
Article Summary: The proposed Constitution (130th Amendment) Bill provides for the automatic removal of the prime minister, chief ministers and ministers from office if they remain in judicial custody for 30 consecutive days. Introduced by Union Home Minister Amit Shah in August 2025, the Bill was referred to a Joint Parliamentary Committee, which is expected to submit its report on July 17.

Article Title: Dead Lizard Found in Mid-Day Meal at West Delhi Govt School, FIR Registered
Article Text:
New Delhi:





Police have registered a case after a dead lizard was found in a mid-day meal container delivered by an NGO at the govt Sarvodaya Bal Vidyalaya co-ed school in West Delhi ’s Hari Nagar Friday.According to police, the school, located in the L-block, usually gets its mid-day meals delivered by NGOs. On the day of the incident, the NGO had reportedly delivered the food in a vehicle around 9 am, after which the administration and staff took possession of it to hand over the packages to the children. The batch of food was reportedly meant for consumption in the upper primary wing.A police officer familiar with the incident said, “The dead lizard was first spotted by the person incharge of handing over the packages to students, who raised an alarm.” Upon noticing the lizard, the Hari Nagar police station was alerted by the school authorities.An FIR was registered in connection with the incident. Police said that no arrests have been made yet and the NGO officials have been questioned to ascertain the sequence of events that led to the animal in the food.
Article Summary: Police have registered a case after a dead lizard was found in a mid-day meal container delivered by an NGO at the govt Sarvodaya Bal Vidyalaya co-ed school in West Delhi. The batch of food was reportedly meant for consumption in the upper primary wing.

Article Title: Maharashtra govt directed to pay Rs 18 lakh for ‘apathetic’ emergency care during labour
Article Text: Mumbai: The Maharashtra State Human Rights Commission (MSHRC) has directed the state govt to pay Rs 17.95 lakh in compensation to a 23-year-old woman’s family for “violating her right to life” by denying her timely emergency care.The commission also noted that the pregnant woman’s delivery was conducted by doctors who were not trained in obstetrics, and that she was shuttled between hospitals due to the absence of doctors and a gynaecologist.The woman, Priyanka Navar, was registered for her delivery at the Watangi primary health centre (PHC) in Kolhapur district’s Ajara taluka and underwentthe required antenatal tests and sonography. Her pregnancy was progressing normally, with the expected date of delivery being Nov 24.Two days before the due date, she went into labour. The doctor at Watangi PHC advised that she be shifted to the Ajara Rural Hospital, but no doctor was available there. She was then brought back to the Watangi PHC, where the delivery took place.According to the family, they were shocked by what they saw inside the labour room. The mother was bleeding profusely, and the staff were unable to control the bleeding.The doctor was absent from the PHC and allegedly instructed on the phone that she be shifted to a private hospital. The private hospital, citing her critical condition, referred her to the govt hospital in Kolhapur, where she died during treatment.In his order, MSHRC member Sanjay Kumar (Retd IPS) noted that when the woman reached the Watangi PHC, the medical officer was absent and her delivery was conducted by two assistant medical officers who were not qualified to manage a critical obstetric emergency. Instead of immediately summoning a doctor, they attempted to manage the case themselves, delaying specialist intervention until the woman’s family raised an alarm. Only then did the doctor concerned reach the PHC.The MSHRC observed: “Although committees constituted to inquire into the alleged negligence, apathy and mismanagement during the delivery of Priyanka Navar gave clean chits to the doctors and medical staff involved, a chronological scrutiny of the incident and the treatment provided to the critically ill pregnant woman by different health institutions and medical officers clearly shows complete apathy, mismanagement and a casual approach in treating a critical patient, ultimately leading to her death. ”
Article Summary: The Maharashtra State Human Rights Commission (MSHRC) has directed the state govt to pay Rs 17.95 lakh in compensation to a 23-year-old woman's family. The commission also noted that the pregnant woman’s delivery was conducted by doctors who were not trained in obstetrics, and that she was shuttled between hospitals due to the absence of doctors.

Article Title: Ram Temple donation theft: Sena (UBT) workers protest in Sambhajinagar
Article Text:
Shiv Sena (UBT) workers protest in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar on Sunday

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:





The local unit of Shiv Sena (UBT) in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, acting on the instructions of the top leadership to protest against the donation theft at the Ram Temple in Ayodhya , on Sunday chanted ‘Ram Raksha’ hymn and staged Ghantanad agitation in front of the famous Supari Hanuman temple.The party workers, who were carrying placards in their hands raised slogans condemning the theft, were led by party MLC Ambadas Danve.Danve said the donation theft at the Ram Temple in Ayodhya is the betrayal of lakhs of Hindu devotees. “The theft is actually treason and traitors must be punished at the earliest,” he said.Former Sena MP Chandrakant Khaire was not part of the local protest. When contacted, his office said Khaire was in Mumbai to take part in the protest organised under the leadership of party chief Uddhav Thackeray The Ayodhya Ram Temple donation theft case allegedly involves a massive financial scandal. The Special Investigation Team (SIT) constituted by the Uttar Pradesh govt has so far arrested eight accused.
Article Summary: The local unit of Shiv Sena (UBT) in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, on Sunday chanted ‘Ram Raksha’ hymn and staged Ghantanad agitation. The party workers, who were carrying placards in their hands raised slogans condemning the theft.

Article Title: ​Panel backed by Mahayuti sweeps district co-op credit bank polls in Sambhajinagar
Article Text:
Member of the winning panel in DCC bank polls

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:





Elections for the District Central Cooperative (DCC) Bank of Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar saw the victory of 11 members of the panel supported by the Mahayuti leaders.While eight members of the panel were already elected unopposed, the Mahayuti-backed panel has won a total 19 out of 20 seats. BJP cabinet minister Atul Save, BJP Phulambri MLA Anuradha Chavan, Shiv Sena Kannad MLA Sanjana Jadhav were among 11 members who recorded victory on Sunday.Barring Jalna Congress MP Kalyan Kale , several candidates from the MVA-backed panel faced defeat in the election. Shiv Sena (UBT) MLC Ambadas Danve who had contested the poll from Mahayuti-backed panel also registered a win on Sunday.Save said chief minister Devendra Fadnavis and deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde will decide the new chairman of the bank. “We formed the Farmers Development Panel with the goal of developing farmers. We won 19 seats. The voter members trusted us and gave valuable support. We will work for the development of the farmers and the bank,” he said.Kale said money power was used by the ruling party leaders in the DCC bank polls. “Still, we have to accept the poll outcome. If the winning panel works in the interest of the farmers and the bank, we will support them. Otherwise, we will oppose by taking to the streets,” he said.On Danve siding with Mahayuti-backed panel, Kale said the Sena (UBT) leader has not followed the alliance dharma.
Article Summary: Mahayuti-backed panel has won a total 19 out of 20 seats in DCC bank polls. 11 members of the panel supported by the Mahayuti leaders recorded victory. Eight members of panel were already elected unopposed. Chief minister Devendra Fadnavis and deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde will decide the new chairman of the bank.

Article Title: Three petrol pump workers booked for duping owner of ₹25 lakh
Article Text:
Pune:





The Talegaon MIDC police have registered a case against three employees of a petrol pump in Ambi village of Maval taluka for allegedly cheating the owner of Rs 25 lakh by diverting customer payments into their personal UPI accounts instead of the petrol pump’s official payment account.According to the police, the accused collected payments from customers through their own UPI IDs and the UPI IDs of other workers between April 1 and May 25 this year. The alleged fraud was uncovered during an internal audit conducted by the petrol pump management. Following the discovery, the owner approached the Talegaon MIDC police and lodged a complaint on Friday.The accused have been booked under sections 318 (cheating) and 316 (criminal breach of trust) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS).A police officer said the three employees had been specifically instructed to accept digital payments only through the petrol pump’s authorised UPI ID. “Instead, they diverted the payments to their personal UPI accounts and those of other workers,” the officer said.To conceal the fraud, the accused allegedly made false entries in the petrol pump’s records and registers, preventing the owner from noticing the discrepancies. Police said the trio misappropriated Rs 25 lakh over nearly two months. Further investigation is underway.
Article Summary: The alleged fraud was uncovered during an internal audit conducted by the petrol pump management. The owner approached the Talegaon MIDC police and lodged a complaint on Friday. The accused have been booked under sections 318 (cheating) and 316 (criminal breach of trust) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita.

Article Title: Man wanted in nationwide marble supply scam arrested from Rajasthan
Article Text: Nagpur: Once himself a victim of cheating, a 12th-pass man from Rajasthan allegedly operated a nationwide marble supply scam that targeted businessmen dealing in high-quality tiles. City police crime branch arrested the accused, Sandeep Mahendra Dan Charan, 29 , a resident of Churu district of Rajasthan, from his native place after a week-long operation.Investigations revealed that Charan is a habitual offender wanted in multiple cases across states. Apart from Nagpur, he faces charges in a cyber police station case in Pune city, Chandgad police station in Kolhapur district, Bhokardan police station in Jalna district and Ratangad police station in Churu, Rajasthan, said police.In the past, city police returned empty-handed from Rajasthan. This time, the crime branch sleuths hired a car with a local registration number and roped in some local people. Undercover cops nabbed Charan from his swanky residence in a remote Rajasthan village.Complainant Raghunath Prasad Kumar Gujar, 57, a resident near Balaji Temple on Besa Road, was allegedly cheated of Rs3.85 lakh between Jan 30 and Feb 10 this year.The FIR was lodged on Feb 17 at Beltarodi police station.Senior inspector Baburao Raut of crime branch said the accused specifically targeted marble businessmen who regularly purchased premium Rajasthan marbles. "Posing as a supplier, he offered lucrative deals before fleeing with the money. His modus operandi involved building trust through calls and digital communications," stated Raut. The operation was monitored by DCP (Detection) Deepak Aggarwal.Acting on senior officers' directives, the crime branch team obtained permissions, traced the accused, conducted medical examination, secured transit remand from Kota and brought Charan to Nagpur. He has been handed over to Beltradi police for further probe.
Article Summary: Sandeep Mahendra Dan Charan, 29, is a habitual offender wanted in multiple cases across states. Undercover cops nabbed Charan from his swanky residence in a remote Rajasthan village. He faces charges in a cyber police station case in Pune city, Chandgad and Kolhapur.

Article Title: Hope Uddhav Recites Ram Raksha Daily: CM Fadnavis
Article Text:
Maharashtra chief minister Devendra Fadnavis on Sunday took a swipe at Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray over his 'Ram Raksha’ agitation,

Chandrapur/Nagpur:





Maharashtra chief minister Devendra Fadnavis on Sunday took a swipe at Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray over his ‘Ram Raksha’ agitation, saying he was glad that Thackeray has at last “remembered Ram”.Speaking to reporters in Chandrapur, Fadnavis welcomed the stir but turned it into a jibe at his former ally. “I am happy that Uddhav Thackeray has finally remembered Lord Ram. Our only expectation was that he should remember Ram,” Fadnavis said.He claimed Thackeray had abandoned the path of Ram, which was why his party has weakened. “If he walks the path of Ram, it will be good for him,” the chief minister said.Fadnavis added that his expectation was that Thackeray recite the Ram Raksha stotra once every day, “not just for today”. Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray on Friday, had announced ‘Ram Raksha’ protest from July 5 over the alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple in Ayodhya . He had said that party workers would gather at a Hanuman temple in Dadar to recite Hanuman Chalisa before demanding that BJP take responsibility for the alleged theft.The alleged embezzlement came to light on June 7. An FIR was registered on June 25 after a preliminary report by a Special Investigation Team (SIT)set up by the Uttar Pradesh govt.Eight people linked to temple’s donation collection, counting have been held so far. Five of them have been seen in CCTV footage; some masking the coverage.
Article Summary: Maharashtra chief minister Devendra Fadnavis on Sunday took a swipe at Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray over his ‘Ram Raksha’ agitation. He said he was glad that Thackerays has at last ‘remembered Ram’ “If he walks the path of Ram, it will be good for him,” he said.

Article Title: Ninth arrest in ₹35 crore bribery case
Article Text:
Ninth arrest in ₹35 crore bribery case





Chennai: Triplicane police on Sunday arrested one more person in connection with the alleged ₹35 crore bribery and intimidation case involving Uthangarai TVK MLA Dr N Ilaiyaraja, taking the total number of arrests to nine. The arrested person was identified as Krishnan, 33, who worked as a driver for Thirunavukkarasu , 45, one of the accused in the case. Thirunavukkarasu was arrested on Wednesday along with Naresh , 40, and Thiyagarajan, 40. Five more persons were arrested the following day in connection with the case. Former minister V Senthil Balaji and his brother V Ashok Kumar have also been named in the case. On Saturday, Triplicane police served summons on the two at their residence in Karur, directing them to appear for an inquiry on Monday. Further investigation is underway.
Article Summary: The arrested person was identified as Krishnan, 33, who worked as a driver for Thirunavukkarasu, 45, one of the accused in the case. Former minister V Senthil Balaji and his brother V Ashok Kumar have also been named. Triplicane police served summons on the two at their residence in Karur.

Article Text:
The contrabands were destroyed in the presence of city police commissioner N Kannan, and deputy commissioners of police G Karthikeyan and P Thangadurai.





COIMBATORE: More than 270 kg of drugs seized from peddlers in Coimbatore city were incinerated at the Kovai Bio-waste Management Facility at Orrattukuppai on Thursday.These contrabands, seized in 140 Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) cases, were incinerated after respective courts directed police to destroy them at a bio-waste treatment facility. They included 270 kg of ganja, 334 gram of methamphetamine, 1.2 kg of ganja chocolates, 39 mg of hashish and 5,168 painkiller tablets.The contrabands were destroyed in the presence of city police commissioner N Kannan, and deputy commissioners of police G Karthikeyan and P Thangadurai.
Article Summary: More than 270 kg of drugs seized from peddlers in Coimbatore city were incinerated at the Kovai Bio-waste Management Facility at Orrattukuppai on Thursday. The contrabands were seized in 140 Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) cases.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: Freed on bail, DMK MLA says TVK govt will fall in six months
Article Text:
DMK MLA ANITHA RADHAKRISHNAN WHO WAS ARRESTED WAS RELEASED ON BAIL BY MAGISTRATE

Tuticorin





: Shortly after being released on bail at midnight on Friday, former minister and DMK Tiruchendur MLA Anitha R Radhakrishnan claimed that the TVK govt led by chief minister C Joseph Vijay would collapse within six months.Speaking to reporters after his release, Radhakrishnan said, “We are not afraid of anyone. Standing by the DMK has always been our history. The dream of defeating us will never come true.”Radhakrishnan was arrested after the Madras high court dismissed his anticipatory bail plea in a case registered under charges of intentional insult to provoke a breach of peace and public mischief.After being questioned for more than five hours, he was produced before a judicial magistrate in Tuticorin shortly after midnight. His counsel sought bail on health grounds and submitted a medical fitness certificate. Accepting the plea, the magistrate granted him bail on the condition that he sign at Authoor police station daily for 10 days.Police said the case relates to a speech delivered by Radhakrishnan at a public meeting in Authoor near Tiruchendur on June 20. Anticipating arrest, he had moved the Madras high court seeking anticipatory bail, but the petition was dismissed on Friday.
Article Summary: DMK Tiruchendur MLA Anitha R Radhakrishnan was arrested after the Madras high court dismissed his anticipatory bail plea. He was arrested on charges of intentional insult to provoke a breach of peace and public mischief. After being questioned for more than five hours, he was produced before a judicial magistrate in Tuticorin.

Article Title: AMMA row: Court directs probe into actor Ansiba’s plaint
Article Text:
Ansiba Hassan

Kochi

:





Judicial first-class magistrate court, Tripunithura, has directed police to investigate a complaint filed by actor Ansiba Hassan alleging illegal detention and harassment.The complaint was filed seeking action against actor Lakshmipriya, her husband Jayesh and Reshma, a sub-inspector. Sources said that the court on Saturday issued the directive after Hassan challenged the police decision to close her police complaint.The actor’s allegation is that she was summoned to Tripunithura Women’s Cell in connection with a complaint lodged by Lakshmipriya. The actor claims that she was made to stay at the police station for nearly three hours and was subjected to harassment by SI Reshma. Hassan alleged that it was a fake case. The woman has also alleged that she was compelled to write and sign an apology at the police station, the content of which was earlier altered.The actor earlier approached the chief minister and the home minister seeking action against those involved. Subsequently, an inquiry conducted by the Thrikkakara assistant commissioner of police reportedly found no merit in the allegations, and hence police closed the complaint. Similarly, the Ernakulam district court directed police to register a case against actor Tini Tom after she challenged the police’s decision not to register an FIR in that case.
Article Summary: Ansiba Hassan filed a complaint alleging illegal detention and harassment. Complaint was filed seeking action against actor Lakshmipriya, her husband Jayesh and Reshma, a sub-inspector. The actor claims that she was made to stay at the police station for nearly three hours and was subjected to harassment.

Article Title: Pakistan air force captain gunned down in broad daylight while rescuing woman
Article Text: Police have launched an investigation and are collecting evidence (Representative image)
Article Summary: Police have launched an investigation and are collecting evidence. Police have launched a probe into the death of a man in a car crash. The incident occurred in the early hours of Monday morning. Police are investigating the incident and collecting evidence at the scene.

Article Title: Days after Pakistan's airstrikes, Afghanistan launches strikes on ISIS targets in Balochistan
Article Text:
'Blatant act of aggression'

Afghanistan's Taliban forces on Wednesday carried out fresh strikes on targets along the border with Pakistan, injuring several people in southwestern Balochistan province.Afghanistan's armed forces claimed that the targeted sites in Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa were hosting bases of Islamic State Khorasan, an affiliate of the Islamic State.According to Afghanistan-based TOLOnews, Afghanistan's defence ministry said that the targeted sites were being used to plan sabotage operations and attacks against civilians inside Afghanistan.The ministry added that the strikes were conducted with precision, inflicting heavy casualties and significant material losses on the group while causing no civilian casualties.Meanwhile, Pakistan's military said that it shot down four rudimentary drones and warned that any further provocation "would receive a befitting response".This comes just days after Pakistan launched airstrikes on Afghanistan, killing at least 28 civilians.However, Pakistan said that those killed in the strikes were militants.Earlier on Monday, India condemned Pakistan's airstrikes in Afghanistan over the weekend, calling them "reckless" and a "blatant act of aggression" that threatened regional peace.In a statement, the foreign ministry said that India "strongly condemns" the Pakistani airstrikes conducted on Afghan territory in the intervening night of Sunday and Monday, which Islamabad claimed targeted terrorist hideouts. The statement said the attack resulted in several civilian casualties, including women and children."This blatant act of aggression by Pakistan is an assault on Afghanistan's sovereignty and a direct threat to regional peace and stability. It reflects Pakistan's persistent pattern of reckless behaviour and its futile attempt to externalise internal failures through desperate acts of violence beyond its borders," the statement read.Tensions have reignited in the region after months of relative calm. The two countries had agreed to a ceasefire in October following weeks of deadly clashes. Intermittent border clashes and air strikes in the area have killed dozens of people in recent months, according to officials in both countries. In February, clashes between the two countries left dozens of people dead.Earlier in June, Pakistan launched deadly air strikes that killed 26 militants. Afghanistan's Taliban government said that 13 people, mostly children, were also killed in the strikes.
Article Summary: Afghanistan's Taliban forces carried out fresh strikes on targets along the border with Pakistan, injuring several people. Afghanistan's armed forces claimed that the targeted sites were hosting bases of Islamic State Khorasan, an affiliate of the Islamic State. Pakistan's military said that it shot down four rudimentary drones and warned that any further provocation "would receive a befitting response"
